description: Someone or something is 'kiranu' when they meet the prerequisites named by a source for a role or activity. A person may hold the required certificate and still be out of practice, so 'pokera' (competent) remains a separate judgement about present performance. 'kanuwa' (authorize) names a grant of permission; qualification says only that the stated list was met.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release it into high-front unrounded 'i'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip for 'r' and open into stressed central 'a'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and finish with high rounded back 'u'.
usage_notes: Nearby clauses identify the role and qualifying source. The quality noun is qualification. An exact credential stays in source form when its issuer, scope, or validity matters.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Keeping the checklist separate from present ability and consent stops an institutional status from answering questions it never tested.

semantic_domains.activity: eligibility for a stated role or activity
semantic_domains.cognition: a comparison with prerequisites named by a source
semantic_domains.community: eligibility defined by a social or institutional source
